attacking_style: Launches multiple attacks on final climbs of medium-mountain stages (typically 20-40km from finish), excels at converting summit attacks into solo victories using superior descending skills.
team_tactics: Operates opportunistically from breakaways rather than relying on team setups; exploits GC team neutralization to target stage wins independently.
terrain_preference: Medium-mountain stages with technical descents after summit finishes; thrives when climbs create selection but GC teams cancel each other out, allowing breakaway freedom.
vulnerability: Appears reliant on breakaway scenarios and opportunistic situations; no evidence of ability to force selection or win when team tactics required.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways when GC teams neutralize each other (2 Vuelta stages, consistent pattern)
Multiple attacks on final climbs before soloing to victory (21km and 40km winning moves)
Exceptional descending converts summit attacks into sustained gaps (both Vuelta wins, 1+ minute margin)
Specialized dominance at Tour de Pologne (12th career stage win there)
Targets medium-mountain stages rather than pure sprint or HC mountain days
form_trajectory: positive: Two Vuelta stage wins in one week (Aug 2017) demonstrate peak form and repeatable winning formula
